title: aglaia rubiginosa
intro: Aglaia rubiginosa, also known as the rusty-bellied aglaia or rusty aglaia, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Meliaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in the tropical regions of Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aglaia rubiginosa include its potential to treat a range of conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Aglaia rubiginosa is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be used in traditional medicine as a paste or powder.
